name: Periodic Review (order-up-to level)
description: Target inventory level for a periodic review system: expected demand over the lead time plus the review period, plus safety stock sized to demand variability over that whole exposure window. At each review, order up to this level.

inputs.name: Service-level factor
inputs.description: Safety factor for the target service level (see reference.service_level_z_factors).

outputs.name: Safety stock
outputs.description: Buffer covering demand variability over the lead time plus the review period.

outputs.name: Order-up-to (target inventory) level
outputs.description: At each review, order the difference between this level and the current inventory position.

expression: SS = z * sigma_d * sqrt(LT + T); TI = D * (LT + T) + SS
assumptions: Reviews happen at a fixed interval T; between reviews no order is placed.
Lead time is constant; only demand varies.
Demand, lead time, and review period use the same time unit.
Lead-time-plus-review demand is approximately normally distributed.
limitations: The exposure window is LT + T (not just LT), so periodic review carries more safety stock than continuous review at the same service level.
If lead time also varies, the combined-variability form should size the buffer instead (see inventory.safety_stock.lead_time_variability).
The order quantity at review is TI minus the current inventory position (on hand + on order), which this record does not compute.

examples.note: 95% service level (z = 1.64), 20 units/day, 5-day lead time, 10-day review cycle, sigma_d = 4. SS = 1.64 x 4 x sqrt(15) = 25.41; TI = 20 x 15 + 25.41 = 325.41.

citations.quote: The formula for calculating the inventory target level is expressed as TI = (D(L + T)) + (SS x sigma_D x sqrt(L + T)) where TI is the target inventory level, D is the demand rate, L is the item lead time, T is the review period, and SS is the safety stock [factor].
There are two critical factors that need to be established on an item-byitem basis: (1) a fixed review interval and (2) an order-up-to target inventory level.
